findings.title: Migration comment misstates what TRUE values become
findings.why_it_matters: Step 3 comment says 'all existing FALSE values become NULL' but USING NULL converts ALL existing values (both TRUE and FALSE) to NULL. If any row had conflict_copy=TRUE (from E93-S01's old duplicate-UUID logic), those rows also become NULL silently. This is likely correct behavior (E93-S01 conflict copies are obsolete) but the comment is incomplete and could mislead future migration authors.
findings.suggested_fix: Change comment to: 'all existing values (TRUE/FALSE) become NULL — old boolean conflict markers from E93-S01 are intentionally cleared'
evidence: Migration line 35 comment: `-- Step 3: Change type from BOOLEAN to JSONB (all existing FALSE values become NULL)`
USING NULL in ALTER COLUMN TYPE converts ALL rows to NULL, not just FALSE ones
E93-S01 migration created conflict_copy as BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE — some rows could theoretically have TRUE if E93-S01's duplicate-UUID logic ran before this migration

residual_risks: The three-step ALTER sequence (DROP NOT NULL → DROP DEFAULT → TYPE CHANGE USING NULL) is correct PostgreSQL procedure for this scenario. Each step is independently valid.
UUID→TEXT cast for conflict_source_id is safe: USING conflict_source_id::TEXT preserves existing UUID strings as text, and NULL values remain NULL.
No RLS policy changes are needed: the conflict columns inherit the existing notes RLS policies (user_id-based row ownership).
No index changes are needed: conflict_copy is a JSONB field, not expected to be indexed at this stage.
testing_gaps: No SQL test or migration test verifies the three-step ALTER works correctly on a table with both TRUE and FALSE conflict_copy values
